parsed_review.summary: CP00-922 is a descriptor-only RP30 People-HR embedded-HRX bridge pack over RP30.P03.M05.S07-RP30.P03.M07.S02 (10 units: 3 ErrorModel security-audit tail rows M05.S07-S09, 5 HRApi implementation re-entry rows M06.S01-S05, 2 EmployeeApi implementation re-entry rows M07.S01-S02). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the descriptor-only boundary is supported and no blocking defect exists. registry.js HRX_CP922_PACK_BINDING and HRX_CP922_REQUIREMENTS match the baseline/manifest exactly (risk A, unit_count 10, first=RP30.P03.M05.S07, last=RP30.P03.M07.S02, upstream CP00-921, next CP00-923, next_subphase RP30.P03.M07.S03, deliverable {implementation:7, security_audit:3}, micro_title {ErrorModel:3, HRApi:5, EmployeeApi:2}); createHrxDomainRows builds every row with descriptor_only:true and all runtime/payload/record-write/policy-execution/real-data/secret/separate-product/runtime-receipt fields hardcoded false (a targeted grep for any of those fields set to true in registry.js and contracts/hrx-people-contract.json returned no matches). createHrxCp922ErrorModelHrEmployeeApiBridgeDescriptor sets source_descriptor=CP921, authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-923), domain_entities ErrorModel/HRApi/EmployeeApi all-false, baseline deferrals true, hr_ai_final_judgment_allowed:false, real-data allowed:false, hermes_packet.emits_runtime_receipt:false, claude_packet.read_only:true/promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, handoff to CP00-923/RP30.P03.M07.S03. validators.js (coverage + descriptor + contract) enforce all those boundaries plus capability/safety-gate/no-leak-guard coverage and reject Claude-as-final-approver and Hermes runtime-receipt emission; model.test.js asserts the CP00-922 distribution and closed boundaries with validation.errors deep-equal []. The generated contracts/hrx-people-contract.json carries current_pack/latest_pack/projection=CP00-922 (HrxCp922ErrorModelHrEmployeeApiBridgeDescriptor), a 3-section/10-row case set (3 ErrorModel + 5 HRApi + 2 EmployeeApi entity rows), validation.valid:true with no drift versus the registry. scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs chains the full CP897->CP922 binding chain + coverage + descriptor + contract + capability/gate checks; all referenced npm scripts exist in package.json; index.js wildcard re-exports the CP922 symbols. closeout-pack-plan.json live_state=CP00-921 with next_subphase RP30.P03.M05.S07 (=CP922 first unit), so CP00-922 is the legitimate queue head; changed-file-scope correctly isolates unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, docx, LDIP review attempt) as preserved and not part of this pack, with no active untracked implementation files omitted. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), the Hermes packet is deterministic review-packet/evidence metadata only (emits_runtime_receipt:false), not embedded in product runtime, and the readiness auth preflight passed (1469-byte valid JSON, is_error:false) with no prior invalid review attempts recorded -- no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced/tool-shaped evidence, or missing fields. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim (runtime_ready:false; manifest/construction-inspection production_ready:false; claude-review-result production_ready:null), so final human approval for enterprise trust correctly remains outside this receipt and does not block this CP closeout goal. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two non-blocking P3s, identical in nature to prior pack CP00-921: (1) pack-level review artifacts are in the sanctioned pre-promotion state and must be normalized from this single review before production_ready flips; (2) command-evidence.json exit codes are agent-reported and were verified here by structural inspection but not re-executed under the read-only constraint. Provenance: implementation and structure were verified directly by source inspection; command pass/fail and the full closeout validation ladder were not re-run in this read-only session. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack closeout or this CP closeout goal.

findings.title: Post-review adjudication, construction inspection, and review receipt normalization still pending
findings.details: At review time the pack-level review artifacts are in their expected pre-promotion state, not a final state: adjudication.md reads 'Overall verdict: PENDING' / 'Production ready after adjudication: no'; construction-inspection.json has production_ready:false with runtime_ready/status/boundary all null; claude-review-result.json status is 'pending_review' with null production_ready/review/findings/dispositions; and manifest.json pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending_review' with overall_verdict 'PENDING', empty valid_review_receipts, empty invalid_review_attempts, and null blocks_pack_closeout/blocks_goal_closeout. No review-receipt.json or raw-output.json exists under artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-922/ yet. This is the sanctioned pre-review state (mirrors CP00-921 finding CP921-P3-01) and not a code or boundary defect, but the pack cannot be promoted to descriptor-only production_ready until closeout automation records exactly one valid hardened read-only review receipt from this review and normalizes the adjudication, construction-inspection, claude-review-result, and manifest review block accordingly.
findings.recommendation: Have closeout automation consume this review receipt, then normalize adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json, and manifest.pack_level_claude_review from this single review (exactly one valid receipt, no blocking P0/P1/P2 findings) and re-run the full deterministic validation ladder before flipping production_ready to true.

findings.title: Closeout command exit codes are agent-reported, not re-executed under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records six commands (node --check on registry/validators/validate script; node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js; npm run rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) all at exit_code 0. Under the read-only review constraint (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) these exit codes were not independently re-executed. Provenance distinction: the implementation and structure were verified directly -- the referenced npm scripts exist in package.json, scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs soundly chains the CP897->CP922 binding/coverage/descriptor/contract ladder, index.js wildcard re-exports the CP922 symbols the script imports, and packages/hrx/test/model.test.js asserts the CP00-922 boundaries with validation.errors deep-equal [] -- but the command pass/fail itself is reported, not re-run in this session. The prompt's full closeout ladder (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) likewise cannot be executed here.
findings.recommendation: Closeout automation with execution rights must run the full validation ladder and capture exit codes before promotion; treat the recorded exit codes as evidence to be re-confirmed by the deterministic harness rather than as reviewer-verified results.
